heading: Marking, labeling, or other identification to show kinds of animals from which derived; separate establishments for preparation and slaughtering activities
label: 619 Marking, labeling, or other identification to show kinds of animals from which derived; separate establishments for preparation and slaughtering activities
text: No person, firm, or corporation shall sell, transport, offer for sale or transportation, or receive for transportation, in commerce, any carcasses of species designated by regulations in effect on the day before , or parts of such carcasses, or the meat or meat food products thereof, unless they are plainly and conspicuously marked or labeled or otherwise identified as required by regulations prescribed by the Secretary to show the kinds of animals from which they were derived. When required by the Secretary, with respect to establishments at which inspection is maintained under this subchapter, such animals and their carcasses, parts thereof, meat and meat food products shall be prepared in establishments separate from those in which other amenable species are slaughtered or their carcasses, parts thereof, meat or meat food products are prepared.November 10, 2005
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to .section 87 of this title

Amendments2005— substituted “species designated by regulations in effect on the day before ,” for “horses, mules, or other equines” and “other amenable species” for “cattle, sheep, swine, or goats”.Pub. L. 109–97November 10, 20051967—, substituted provisions for marking, labeling, or other identification of kinds of animals whence the articles are derived and for separate establishments for preparation and slaughtering activities for prohibition against transportation or sale of meat or meat food products without complying with inspection provisions. See .Pub. L. 90–201, § 9section 610(b) of this title

Effective Date of 2005 AmendmentAmendment by effective the day after 120 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 109–97section 798(b) of Pub. L. 109–97section 601 of this titleNov. 10, 2005
